title: ballota nigra subsp. ruderalis
intro: Ballota nigra subsp. ruderalis, also known as wild bergamot or bee balm, is a subspecies of the Ballota nigra plant, which is a perennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily its essential oil, which contains a high concentration of linalool and camphor, and has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, anxiety, and skin problems. The health benefits of Ballota nigra subsp. ruderalis include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
